title: Obstruction Efficiency FAIL: class 4 (valid) — false pruning claimed, pruning_ratio=1.0 is wrong
_fixture_purpose: FAIL fixture: the aware planner incorrectly prunes a valid target (class 4, v_3(4)=0, OBSTRUCTION_ABSENT). The cert declares aware_trace.pruned_before_search=true and efficiency_claim.pruning_ratio=1.0. This is doubly wrong: (1) pruning a valid target is a FALSE_PRUNING_EFFICIENCY error — the planner has incorrectly blocked a reachable goal; (2) the efficiency_claim is inconsistent with OBSTRUCTION_ABSENT (aware_trace.pruned_before_search must be false when obstruction is absent). The validator catches both: AWARE_TRACE_MISMATCH (pruned=true on OBSTRUCTION_ABSENT) and FALSE_PRUNING_EFFICIENCY (false_pruning=true on OBSTRUCTION_ABSENT target).

baseline_trace.notes: Naive planner: expanded 3 nodes, found 2-move plan (increase_gain → apply_lowpass).

aware_trace.notes: INCORRECTLY pruned: v_3(4)=0 means OBSTRUCTION_ABSENT, but planner applied prune anyway. This is a planner bug — it pruned a valid, reachable target.

efficiency_claim.notes: Pruning ratio 1.0 is spurious: the 3 'saved' nodes would have found a valid plan. This is not an efficiency gain — it is a correctness failure. false_pruning=true flags the error.

validation_checks.details: saved_nodes=3; 3-0=3; arithmetic match (but savings are spurious)
pruning_ratio=1.0; 3/3=1.0; arithmetic match (but ratio is spurious)
false_pruning=true — valid target was pruned — FALSE_PRUNING_EFFICIENCY

fail_ledger.message: aware_trace.pruned_before_search=true but obstruction_verdict=OBSTRUCTION_ABSENT: planner must not prune when v_p(r)=0.
efficiency_claim.false_pruning=true: the planner pruned a valid (non-forbidden) target class 4. Pruning a reachable goal is a correctness failure, not an efficiency gain.
